sections.title: Authentication vs Authorization
points: Authorization = determines permissions (what you can do).
Example: User can view S3 bucket but cannot delete it.

sections.title: AWS Shared Responsibility Model
points: AWS manages hardware, networking, Regions, data centers.
Customers manage data, IAM, configurations, OS patches on EC2, applications.

sections.title: IAM Core Concepts
points: Groups = collection of users with shared permissions.
Roles = temporary access with assumed permissions.
Policies = JSON documents defining Allow or Deny.

sections.title: Least Privilege + Root User
points: Least Privilege = give only minimum permissions needed.
Root user has full account access.
Use strong password + enable MFA on root.
Do not use root for daily tasks.

sections.title: Network Protection
points: ELB distributes traffic and helps absorb spikes.
AWS Shield Standard = automatic common DDoS protection.

sections.title: AWS WAF
points: Blocks malicious IPs, SQL injection, XSS.

sections.title: Encryption Basics
points: At Rest = stored data (S3, EBS, RDS, DynamoDB).
In Transit = moving data over network.

sections.title: Data Protection Services
points: ACM = AWS Certificate Manager for SSL/TLS certificates.
Macie = discovers sensitive data in Amazon S3.
S3 supports encryption by default for new objects.

sections.title: Threat Detection Services
points: Inspector = scans vulnerabilities on EC2, containers, Lambda.
GuardDuty = threat detection using logs + ML.
Security Hub = central dashboard for findings.
